nonplanar_enabled.label: Non-Planar Slicing
nonplanar_enabled.description: Enable non-planar curved layers on inclined surfaces to eliminate stair-stepping artifacts. The mesh is deformed before slicing and toolpaths are back-transformed, producing genuinely curved infill and walls. Only applies to regions verified collision-free with the printhead.

nonplanar_max_angle.label: Non-Planar Maximum Angle
nonplanar_max_angle.description: Maximum surface angle from horizontal that can be printed non-planarly (degrees). Higher values improve surface quality on steeper surfaces but increase collision risk. Limited by nozzle/fan geometry.

nonplanar_nozzle_clearance.label: Non-Planar Nozzle Clearance
nonplanar_nozzle_clearance.description: Distance from the nozzle tip to the first obstruction above it (fan shroud, sensor, etc.) in mm. This is used for collision detection. A safety margin is subtracted internally.

nonplanar_min_region_area.label: Non-Planar Minimum Region Area
nonplanar_min_region_area.description: Minimum surface area (mm²) of a candidate region for non-planar slicing. Regions smaller than this are kept planar to avoid unnecessary processing.

nonplanar_blend_distance.label: Non-Planar Blend Distance
nonplanar_blend_distance.description: Width of the smooth transition zone (mm) between planar and non-planar regions. Prevents abrupt layer height changes at boundaries.

nonplanar_heightmap_resolution.label: Non-Planar Height Map Resolution
nonplanar_heightmap_resolution.description: Grid cell size (mm) for the surface height map used in analysis. Smaller values give more precise surface following but increase memory usage and analysis time.

nonplanar_safety_margin.label: Non-Planar Safety Margin
nonplanar_safety_margin.description: Additional clearance (mm) subtracted from the nozzle clearance during collision checking. Higher values are more conservative.

nonplanar_min_benefit_angle.label: Non-Planar Minimum Benefit Angle
nonplanar_min_benefit_angle.description: Minimum surface angle from horizontal (degrees) for non-planar treatment to be worthwhile. Surfaces flatter than this show negligible stair-stepping and are kept planar.

nonplanar_field_decay_mm.label: Non-Planar Deformation Decay Distance
nonplanar_field_decay_mm.description: Controls how deep below the surface the layer curving extends (mm). Small values (1-2mm) only curve the topmost layers. Larger values (5-20mm) produce smoother transitions affecting more layers, with genuinely curved infill throughout.

nonplanar_min_thickness_ratio.label: Non-Planar Min Layer Thickness Ratio
nonplanar_min_thickness_ratio.description: Minimum allowed layer thickness as a fraction of the nominal layer height. Prevents layers from becoming too thin after deformation.

nonplanar_max_thickness_ratio.label: Non-Planar Max Layer Thickness Ratio
nonplanar_max_thickness_ratio.description: Maximum allowed layer thickness as a fraction of the nominal layer height. Prevents layers from becoming too thick after deformation.

nonplanar_optimization_resolution.label: Non-Planar Deformation Grid Resolution
nonplanar_optimization_resolution.description: Grid cell size (mm) for computing the deformation field. Larger values are faster but less precise. The deformation field is interpolated to the height map resolution for path transformation.
